title: Hive
objectives: Use Hive boxes as a fast local KV store
Register type adapters for custom classes
Know when Hive is and isn't the right fit

sections.markdown: Hive is a pure-Dart, lightweight NoSQL key-value store: data lives in **boxes** (a Map<String, dynamic> on steroids). No native code, fast on device, no queries — just get/put.
Hive shines for **app preferences, small caches, and simple lookups** where SQLite/Isar are overkill. For any structured data with queries or joins, reach for Drift or Isar.
Storing thousands of large objects and filtering in Dart. Hive isn't a query engine — full scans on big datasets tank your UI. Add a real DB when you need queries.
Encrypt sensitive boxes with `HiveAesCipher`. Register adapters BEFORE opening boxes. Use lazy boxes for large values you don't read on startup.

flashcards.back: A persistent Map-like container for key-value data, backed by binary files on device.

flashcards.front: How do you store a custom class?
flashcards.back: Register a TypeAdapter (generated via hive_generator + build_runner).
When you need queries, indexes, joins, or gigabytes of structured data.

options: A key-value store in pure Dart
quiz.explain: Fast local KV store, no native code.

quiz.q: To store custom classes you need…
quiz.explain: TypeAdapters teach Hive how to serialize your class.

quiz.q: Hive is a poor fit for…
quiz.explain: No query engine — full scans get expensive fast.

challenges.text: Persist a theme setting with Hive.
Store a custom User class via a TypeAdapter.
Encrypt a box with HiveAesCipher and rotate the key.
Migrate from Hive to Isar/Drift when your data outgrows KV.
